texto: That to me was the most poignant part of Diana's wedding; as she was walking up the aisle and her eyes were going left to right, looking at people and smiling in the way that Diana did - and that diamond tiara glittering like mad. It was great.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bruce Oldfield: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “That to me was the most poignant part of Diana's wedding; as she was walking up the aisle and her…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, smiling, great pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e great.

frase_original: That to me was the most poignant part of Diana's wedding; as she was walking up the aisle and her eyes were going left to right, looking at people and smiling in the way that Diana did - and that diamond tiara glittering like mad. It was great.
